About Us and About our Platform

At our core, we are a business growth platform built to simplify the way modern businesses operate, market, and scale. We understand the frustration of juggling multiple subscriptions, disconnected software, expensive tools, and complicated systems just to run a business efficiently. That’s why we set out to build a single platform that brings everything together in one powerful ecosystem.

Our platform combines the essential tools businesses need to grow — including CRM management, funnel building, email marketing, SMS campaigns, automation workflows, lead capture, calendar bookings, customer communication, sales pipelines, reporting, and much more. Instead of paying for multiple services that don’t communicate properly with each other, our users can manage everything from one central workspace designed for simplicity, performance, and scalability.


What makes our platform different is the flexibility we provide. Businesses can choose from ready-made plans or build a completely customised setup based on their exact requirements. Whether you are a startup, local business, agency, online brand, or growing enterprise, our system is designed to scale with you as your business evolves.

We’ve focused heavily on creating a platform that is not only powerful, but also easy to use. Many business tools are overloaded with complexity, hidden fees, and unnecessary features. We believe software should help businesses grow faster — not slow them down. Every feature we build is designed to save time, improve efficiency, automate repetitive tasks, and help businesses generate more leads, sales, and customer engagement.

Our mission is simple: to provide businesses with affordable, scalable technology that delivers real results without the overwhelming costs of managing multiple platforms. We are continuously developing new tools, integrations, and features to ensure our users always have access to modern solutions that help them stay ahead in an increasingly digital world.

Powerful Modules. One Connected Platform.

Our platform brings together powerful business tools designed to help you automate operations, increase productivity, generate more leads, and grow your business from one central ecosystem. Every module works independently while also integrating seamlessly together, giving businesses complete flexibility and control.

Whether you need a CRM, marketing automation, funnels, websites, bookings, social media management, communities, or communication tools, every module has been designed to simplify workflows, improve efficiency, and reduce the need for multiple disconnected software subscriptions.

From small businesses and tradies through to growing teams and larger organisations, our platform delivers the tools needed to manage customers, automate marketing, improve communication, and scale operations through intelligent AI-powered systems and streamlined business automation.

Explore our powerful range of modules below and discover how each system has been built to work together to create one complete business operating platform.

cards.description:
The Command Centre gives you complete control of your business from one powerful central dashboard designed to simplify daily operations and improve productivity.

Monitor live business statistics, manage priorities, track performance, and stay organised with intelligent task management and real-time insights all in one place.

Everything you need is accessible from a single streamlined interface, helping your team work smarter, faster, and more efficiently every day.

Track conversions, automate follow-ups, and optimise every stage of your customer journey to generate more leads and increase sales.

props.text:
EMAIL MARKETING

Email marketing remains one of the most powerful and profitable marketing channels available to any business. While social media platforms can be excellent for building awareness and attracting attention, the reality is that you don't own your followers. Algorithms change, accounts can be restricted, reach can be reduced overnight, and your audience ultimately belongs to the platform, not your business.

That's why experienced marketers often say, "The money is in the list." Every time you collect an email address, you're building a valuable business asset that you own and control. Unlike social media followers, your email database gives you direct access to your audience whenever you want to communicate with them.

Businesses that focus solely on social media without capturing email addresses are often leaving significant revenue on the table. You're spending time, effort, and advertising dollars attracting potential customers, only to let many of them disappear without any way to follow up. By capturing leads and nurturing them through email marketing, you can continue building relationships, providing value, and generating sales long after their first interaction with your business.

Email marketing allows you to stay connected with your audience, promote products and services, announce special offers, educate customers, and build long-term loyalty. Combined with automation and autoresponders, it becomes a powerful system that works for your business around the clock, helping turn enquiries into customers and customers into lifelong advocates for your brand.

Automated email responders that nurture your leads 24/7

Set up powerful autoresponder sequences that engage your audience from the moment they sign up. Deliver the right message at the right time without lifting a finger.

Email autoresponders are one of the most powerful tools a business can use to build stronger customer relationships and improve client retention. By automatically delivering the right message at the right time, businesses can stay connected with customers long after the initial enquiry, purchase, or interaction.

contentBlocks.text: BROADCASTS
Reach your entire list with one powerful send

Launch targeted broadcast campaigns to your full list or segmented audiences. Track opens, clicks, and conversions in real time with beautiful analytics.

Email broadcasts are perfect for announcements, promotions, newsletters, product launches, and important updates. Deliver your message directly to your subscribers' inboxes and reach thousands of contacts instantly.
EMAIL CAMPAiGNS
End-to-end campaigns that convert

Plan, design, and execute complete email campaigns with our drag-and-drop builder. A/B test subject lines, preview on all devices, and optimise for maximum ROI.

Successful email marketing is more than sending messages—it’s about creating strategic campaigns that guide your audience toward action. Build targeted campaigns with professional templates, compelling content, audience segmentation, and performance tracking.
Build Once. Automate Forever.

Create intelligent email sequences that automatically engage leads, nurture prospects, and convert customers while you focus on growing your business.

Email automation allows you to deliver the right message at exactly the right time without lifting a finger. Welcome new subscribers, follow up on enquiries, nurture prospects through the buying journey, recover abandoned opportunities, and re-engage inactive customers automatically. With powerful triggers, conditions, and workflows, your business can stay connected with every lead and customer 24/7 while saving countless hours of manual work.

props.rightContent:
A CRM (Customer Relationship Management) system is one of the most valuable tools a growing business can implement. Many businesses still rely on spreadsheets, notebooks, email inboxes, and memory to manage customer relationships, leads, quotes, and follow-ups. While this may work in the early stages, it quickly becomes difficult to maintain consistency, resulting in missed opportunities, forgotten follow-ups, and lost revenue.

The Gr8 Result CRM is designed to centralise every customer interaction in one easy-to-manage platform. Every lead, customer enquiry, quote, phone call, email, SMS conversation, task, and appointment can be stored and tracked from a single dashboard. This gives business owners and their teams complete visibility over their sales pipeline, ensuring no lead falls through the cracks and every opportunity is followed up at the right time.

One of the biggest advantages of the Gr8 Result CRM is automation. Instead of manually chasing leads, setting reminders, or sending follow-up emails, the system can automate these tasks for you. Automated workflows, email campaigns, SMS reminders, and task scheduling help businesses respond faster, nurture prospects more effectively, and convert more enquiries into paying customers. The result is increased efficiency, improved customer experiences, and significantly higher conversion rates.

For businesses that have never used a CRM before, the impact can be immediate. Teams become more organised, communication improves, and management gains real-time visibility into sales performance. Business owners can instantly see where leads are coming from, which team members are performing best, which marketing campaigns are generating results, and where opportunities are being lost. This level of insight allows businesses to make smarter decisions based on real data rather than guesswork.

Unlike traditional CRM systems that can be complex and difficult to implement, the Gr8 Result CRM has been built with simplicity and usability in mind. Its intuitive interface allows businesses to get up and running quickly while still providing the powerful tools needed to scale. Whether you're a small business looking to improve lead management or a growing company needing advanced automation and reporting, the Gr8 Result CRM provides the foundation for sustainable growth.

Ultimately, businesses that use a CRM close more sales, deliver better customer service, and operate more efficiently than those that don't. The Gr8 Result CRM gives businesses the tools, automation, and visibility they need to turn more enquiries into customers, retain those customers for longer, and create predictable, scalable growth.

props.text: TRAFFIC TO LEAD TO CRM TO FOLLOW-UP TO SALE TO REPEAT BUSINESS

A funnel is not just a page. It is a guided sales system.

Most businesses lose leads because visitors are given too many choices, no clear next step, and no automatic follow-up after they leave. A funnel fixes that by focusing every page, form, message, offer and automation around one conversion goal.

With Gr8 Result Digital Solutions, a funnel can connect your landing pages, lead magnets, forms, calendar bookings, quotes, email marketing, SMS follow-ups, CRM pipelines, phone activity and reporting into one connected journey. The result is simple: more enquiries captured, faster follow-up, better sales conversations, and fewer opportunities slipping through the cracks.

props.text:
The Gr8 Result Funnel Map

A strong funnel is not a random collection of pages. It is a connected path where every action triggers the next useful step.

1. Attract

Ads, social posts, organic content, referrals and email traffic land on a focused campaign page.

2. Capture

Forms, offers, bookings and quote requests collect the details needed to continue the conversation.

3. Nurture

Email, SMS, task reminders and call prompts keep the lead moving while interest is highest.

4. Convert

The CRM tracks the opportunity through booking, quote, proposal, sale, review and repeat business.

props.title: Funnel Questions Business Owners Usually Ask
props.subtitle: Simple answers for teams that want more leads without building a confusing technology stack.

items.question: What is the difference between a website and a funnel?
items.answer: A website gives visitors many paths to explore. A funnel is built around one focused outcome, such as booking a call, requesting a quote, downloading a guide or joining a campaign. Both matter, but funnels are designed for measurable conversion.

items.question: Can a funnel connect to CRM, email and SMS?
items.answer: Yes. That is the point. A lead should not sit in an inbox. Funnel activity can feed the CRM, trigger email and SMS follow-up, create tasks, segment contacts and help your team see which campaigns are producing real opportunities.

items.question: Can we build different funnels for different services?
items.answer: Yes. A builder might need quote funnels for renovations, booking funnels for consultations, and reactivation funnels for old enquiries. A coach might need webinar, course and application funnels. The structure should match the offer.

items.question: Do we need every feature on day one?
items.answer: No. The best approach is to launch a clear first version, measure the result, then improve it. The page has been written to show where the platform can go as your funnel tools expand.

props.text:
The Hidden Cost of Managing Projects Across Five Disconnected Tools

Most trade, construction and service businesses are losing tens of thousands of dollars every year to a problem they have completely normalised. Their quoting tool does not talk to their scheduling tool. Their scheduling tool does not talk to their job board. Their job board has no connection to their documents folder. Their documents folder is not linked to their communication history. And none of it feeds reliable information into their accounting or reporting systems. Every handover between these disconnected systems is an opportunity for scope to be misunderstood, deadlines to slip, variations to disappear and owners to lose visibility over what is actually happening on their jobs.

The administrative overhead alone is staggering. Scope gets retyped from the quote into the project plan. Dates are manually entered into the scheduling tool after the project is confirmed. Client information is duplicated across the CRM, the estimating software and the project management app. Variations are agreed informally and sometimes invoiced and sometimes forgotten. Reports are built at the end of every month by pulling data from five different places and presenting it in yet another document that is already out of date by the time it is read. Staff spend hours every week moving information between tools that were never designed to work together and the business pays the cost of that wasted time in every single project.

Project Hub exists to end that fragmentation permanently. It gives growing businesses the connected operational platform they need to run projects like the best-managed companies in their industry, without paying enterprise software prices, without forcing the team through months of painful implementation and without accepting that disconnected tools are simply the cost of doing business. The game has changed. Your project management platform should change with it.

props.title: What disconnected project management actually costs your business
props.subtitle: These are not abstract numbers. They are the real, compounding costs paid by every project-based business running jobs across a fragmented stack of tools that were never designed to work together.

stats.label: Admin waste per project
stats.detail: Hours spent re-entering data, chasing status updates, reformatting quotes and manually rebuilding reports across disconnected systems that should share the same data automatically.

stats.label: Margin lost to scope creep
stats.detail: Variations agreed informally and never logged properly erode profit on almost every project-based job. Without a connected commercial record, scope changes disappear into conversation threads.

stats.label: Average disconnected stack
stats.detail: Most growing businesses pay separately for quoting software, a task management app, a cloud storage folder, a scheduling tool and a calendar application that were never designed to communicate with each other.

stats.label: Delay cost per missed milestone
stats.detail: A blocked dependency that goes unseen for even a few days can push a completion date by weeks and a cash position by thousands. Without connected visibility, the problem is discovered too late to fix cheaply.

stats.label: Monthly reporting time
stats.detail: Without connected data, building a monthly report means pulling information from multiple sources, reconciling inconsistencies and presenting a document that represents the past rather than the present.

stats.label: Single source of truth
stats.detail: When the project record lives across five tools, no single version of the truth exists. Decisions get made with incomplete information and accountability becomes impossible to maintain.

props.title: The operational command centre your business has been missing
props.leftTitle: Why Project Hub changes how work actually gets done
props.leftContent:
Every successful project-based business eventually hits the same ceiling. The team is working hard, the owner is staying late, jobs are being delivered — but the margins are tighter than they should be, communication keeps slipping, deadlines are being pushed and the business feels harder to manage than it ought to. The cause is almost always the same: the information needed to run the business is fragmented across too many systems that were never designed to share data with each other.

Project Hub is the answer to that fragmentation. It is the layer of the GR8 Result platform specifically designed to connect the commercial side of a job with the operational side. The quote that was sold, the plan that was made, the tasks that were assigned, the documents that were shared, the updates that were sent and the reports the owner needs should not live in five different places. They should all belong to the same connected project record, accessible by everyone who needs them, updated in real time and trusted as the one true picture of the job.

When that connection exists, the business changes in ways that are immediately felt. Administrative overhead drops because data is captured once and flows everywhere it is needed. Deadline visibility improves because the full timeline is in one place rather than spread across a calendar, a spreadsheet and someone's memory. Team accountability becomes real because tasks have owners, due dates and status that everyone can see. Clients get better answers because your team can speak from a live operational picture rather than a verbal estimate. Owners stop spending their mornings chasing information and start making decisions with confidence.

That is the promise of Project Hub — and it is built into the same platform that handles your marketing, CRM, email, SMS, automation, website builder and reporting. The business that runs its projects on GR8 Result is the business that finally operates from one source of truth.

props.title: Estimate Builder: the commercial foundation of every project
props.leftTitle: Build quotes that protect your margin and convert directly into live projects without re-entering a single piece of data
props.leftContent:
The Estimate Builder is not a price calculator you use once and forget about. It is the commercial engine at the heart of Project Hub — the system that determines whether a job starts with accurate scope, realistic labour assumptions, properly priced materials and the margin protection your business depends on. Every detail entered during the quoting process becomes the foundation of the project that follows, which means the quality of the estimate directly determines the quality of the delivery.

This matters because the biggest source of project problems is the gap between what was quoted and what the delivery team was actually told to do. When the estimate lives in a quoting tool and the project plan lives in a separate system, that gap opens up immediately after approval. Labour assumptions get lost in translation. Material quantities become vague references rather than specific scope items. Variation accountability disappears into email threads that nobody can find months later. Margin that was built into the original price quietly evaporates between systems that do not talk to each other.

Project Hub closes that gap permanently. When a client approves the proposal, the commercial information flows directly into the project workspace. The scope that was priced becomes the basis for the Gantt timeline. The labour assumptions inform the scheduling. The materials context sits next to the procurement tasks on the Job Board. That connected handover from quote to delivery is one of the biggest operational advantages Project Hub offers over competitors who treat estimating and project management as entirely separate workflows that need separate software.

Beyond the numbers, the Estimate Builder supports client-ready proposals with professional structure, cover pages, terms and conditions, optional extras presented clearly and variation tracking built in from the beginning. Your team sends a document that reflects the quality of the business, tracks whether the client has opened it and can follow up with a clear next step — all without leaving the platform.

  • Itemised line items with quantities and unit costs
  • Labour rates, allowances and role-based costing
  • Material costs connected to specific scope items
  • Markup and margin controls at line and total level
  • Provisional sums, optional extras and staged pricing
  • Variation tracking built into the commercial record
  • Client-ready proposal with approval workflow
  • Approved quotes convert directly into live project workspaces

props.title: Estimator capabilities that protect profit before the job ever begins

items.title: Labour visibility that survives the handover
items.body:
Most margin leakage on project-based jobs happens in labour, not materials. Estimates are built with one set of labour assumptions and delivered with another because nothing connected the two systems. Project Hub keeps labour allowances, role types, rates and cost assumptions visible from quote right through to delivery so the team executing the job can see exactly where the margins were set and how the current performance compares to those original assumptions.

When labour thinking is preserved through the handover rather than lost in translation, the business has a genuine chance of finishing the job at the margin it was originally priced at. That is a structural advantage that most businesses with disconnected tools never achieve.

items.title: Materials with real scope accountability
items.body:
Material quantities and costs need to be tied to specific scope items, not buried in a single line total that means nothing to the person doing procurement. When the Estimate Builder connects materials to the work being priced, the person responsible for ordering supplies understands exactly what was included in the quote, what quantities were assumed, what the cost expectations are and where the risk sits if prices have moved since the estimate was prepared.

That connection reduces over-ordering, under-ordering and the uncomfortable conversations that happen when delivery costs more than the quote anticipated because nobody checked what was actually included in the original price.

items.title: Markup, margin and variation control that actually works
items.body:
Margins are easy to protect when the commercial structure is transparent and variations are treated as part of the official record rather than informal side agreements. Project Hub supports markup and margin settings that apply across the entire estimate so pricing decisions are deliberate and documented. When a client requests additional scope, that variation enters the commercial record with its own pricing, approval status and connection to the original quote.

Every variation that is agreed, requested, declined or pending should have a paper trail. When it does not, that is money the business is giving away without even knowing it. Variation control is one of the highest-impact commercial capabilities in any real-world project management system.

props.title: Gantt Charts: turn complex delivery into a timeline your whole team can follow
props.rightTitle: Plan dependencies, milestones and the critical path before delays cost you money
props.rightContent:
Most project failures do not happen because the team stopped trying. They happen because the schedule was never properly planned, the dependencies between tasks were not visible and the first delay was not caught early enough to prevent it cascading into something far more expensive. The Gantt Chart in Project Hub is designed to solve that problem at its source — not after the damage has already been done.

Visual timeline planning gives your team and your clients a shared understanding of the work. Every phase, task, milestone and deadline is mapped so that the relationship between activities is immediately clear. When Task B cannot start until Task A is finished, that dependency is visible in the chart. When a dependent task slips, the team can see which downstream tasks are now at risk and respond before the impact reaches the client relationship or the final completion date.

Critical path visibility is one of the most powerful capabilities in professional project management. The critical path is the sequence of tasks that directly controls when the project finishes. When any task on the critical path is delayed, the project end date moves. When the critical path is visible in the system, the team knows precisely where to focus protective attention to keep the timeline intact. Most project management tools show you a nice chart. Project Hub shows you where the real risk is hiding.

Resource planning is the other major advantage of proper Gantt scheduling. When you can see who is assigned to what, when their capacity is fully allocated and when multiple tasks are competing for the same person or equipment, you can make smarter scheduling decisions before the conflict reaches the job site. That is the difference between reactive project management and genuine operational control that protects both delivery dates and client relationships.

  • Full timeline view across all project phases
  • Task dependencies and predecessor relationships
  • Critical path identification and monitoring
  • Milestone markers with accountability owners
  • Resource allocation across the team
  • Delay visibility before it cascades
  • Progress tracking against the original plan
  • Connected to the Job Board for daily execution alignment

props.title: Scheduling intelligence for projects that change in the real world

items.title: Dependencies that make risk visible before it becomes expensive
items.body:
Dependencies are the relationships between tasks that determine whether the schedule will hold when reality deviates from the plan. When Task B cannot start until Task A is complete, and Task A slips by three days, the team needs to understand that impact immediately — not after the schedule has already unravelled and the client is asking uncomfortable questions about the new completion date.

Project Hub makes dependency chains visible so project managers can think ahead. They can see the knock-on effect of a single delay, communicate it to stakeholders before it becomes a crisis and adjust the plan while there are still options available. That early visibility is worth more than any other scheduling feature, because it is the difference between managing a problem and being managed by it.

items.title: Milestones that create real accountability across the team
items.body:
Milestones mark the moments in a project that matter most. They are the dates clients measure progress against, the checkpoints that trigger staged payments, the events that define whether a project is running to plan or quietly falling behind schedule. When milestones are built into the Gantt structure rather than written on a whiteboard, accountability follows automatically.

Project Hub builds milestones into the operational plan so they are part of the live schedule, visible to the whole team and connected to the specific tasks that determine whether they will be achieved on time. When a milestone is at risk, the system shows exactly which task is creating that risk.

items.title: Progress owners can understand without calling a meeting
items.body:
Project visibility should not require a status meeting. When the Gantt chart is maintained as part of the daily workflow rather than updated sporadically before a client review, owners and managers can look at the plan at any moment and immediately understand which phases are complete, which are in progress, which are delayed and which are approaching risk territory.

That transparency reduces time spent on update calls, status requests and internal progress meetings. It also builds significantly stronger trust with clients. When a project manager can speak confidently about exactly where the job stands, backed by a live operational picture rather than a verbal estimate from memory, the client relationship becomes more professional and more resilient when things change.

props.title: Job Board: the live operational centre for every active job in the business
props.leftTitle: Move work through the business without losing context, accountability or momentum
props.leftContent:
The Job Board is where the project plan becomes daily execution. It is the place your team comes to understand what needs to happen today, what they are currently working on, what is blocked, what is waiting on someone else and what has been successfully completed. Without a clear, shared, real-time view of the work queue, jobs stall at handover points, accountability disappears between team members and supervisors spend most of their day chasing people for updates instead of actually managing the business and protecting its margins.

Project Hub's Job Board organises every task and every job into visual stages that the whole team can see and interact with. Work moves through customisable kanban columns — from pending through in-progress, waiting on external, under review and complete — using simple drag-and-drop task management that every team member can master in minutes. Each task card carries the full context the assigned person needs: who owns it, when it is due, what priority level it is, what files are attached, what comments have been added and what the current status is in the broader project timeline.

The most valuable aspect of a well-designed job board is not just what it shows when everything is working. It is what it reveals when something is wrong. Tasks sitting in the wrong column for too long. Work that has been waiting for a client approval for three days. Stages that have accumulated too many items because upstream work is bottlenecked. These are early warning signals that the job needs attention and management intervention. Project Hub makes those signals impossible to miss.

For businesses managing multiple active projects simultaneously — construction companies running four sites, trade businesses juggling six concurrent jobs, commercial fit-out teams handling different client schedules — the Job Board provides the visibility that makes multi-project management possible without the chaos. Every job has its own board, every task has a clear owner and the overall business picture is always available without needing to call a meeting or wait for someone to compile a report.

  • Kanban-style visual task stages
  • Drag-and-drop task movement between stages
  • Ownership assignments with due dates and priority levels
  • File attachments directly on task cards
  • Threaded comments and update history
  • Status visibility for owners, supervisors and team members
  • Multi-project overview across all active jobs
  • Connected to the Gantt timeline for schedule alignment

props.title: Job Board features that keep teams moving and owners properly informed

items.title: Assignments with genuine ownership and accountability
items.body:
A task without a clear owner is a task that will probably not get done — or will get done poorly because no single person felt fully responsible for its outcome. Assignments in Project Hub remove the ambiguity that causes work to stall between office staff, site supervisors and management teams. Every task has one owner, a due date and a priority level so the responsibility is absolutely clear from the moment the task is created to the moment it is completed.

When someone is assigned a task in Project Hub, they know it is their responsibility. When a due date approaches, it is visible to the whole team. When a task becomes overdue, it stands out. That simple, unambiguous clarity eliminates one of the most common and costly sources of delivery failure in project-based businesses of every size.

items.title: Files and comments where the actual work happens
items.body:
Project context should live near the project work — not in a separate email thread, not in a WhatsApp group and not in a shared drive folder with seventeen versions of the same document. When photos, approval documents, specifications, site plans, subcontractor quotes and client sign-offs are attached directly to the relevant task in Project Hub, the person doing that work has everything they need without wasting time searching through unrelated conversations and cloud folders.

Comments on task cards create a conversation thread that belongs permanently to the work itself. When a supervisor needs to understand why something was done a particular way three months later, the answer is in the task history. When a client disputes an aspect of the delivery, the documentation is attached to the relevant card with timestamps. That record reduces misunderstandings and protects the business in the conversations that matter most.

items.title: Status columns that make bottlenecks immediately visible
items.body:
The most valuable thing about a well-designed job board is not what it shows when the team is performing well and work is flowing smoothly. It is what it reveals when something is breaking down and needs immediate management attention. Tasks stuck in the wrong column, approval requests waiting days without response, stages with accumulated items because upstream work has bottlenecked — all early warning signals that the job is at risk.

Project Hub's status columns make those warning signals impossible to ignore. A supervisor or business owner looking at the board for sixty seconds can see whether the team is flowing, blocked or falling behind. That visibility creates the ability to intervene early rather than discovering the problem after the deadline has already been missed and the client relationship is under strain.

props.title: Traditional project management vs GR8 Result Project Hub
props.leftTitle: How most project-based businesses are operating right now
props.leftContent:
A new lead comes in and gets entered into the CRM. Then the quote is built in a separate estimating tool. When the quote is approved, it gets manually summarised in an email to the project manager who creates a new entry in the project management app. The team is briefed in a kick-off meeting that covers ground the estimate already captured. Tasks are distributed via email, WhatsApp or verbal instruction. Files go into a shared cloud folder that half the team cannot find. Updates come through calls and text messages. Variations are discussed and sometimes logged and sometimes not. Reports are assembled manually at the end of every month by pulling data from the quoting tool, the project app, the accounting software and the email history.

Every handover between those steps is an opportunity for information to be lost, duplicated, misunderstood or simply not transferred at all. Every additional tool is a subscription cost and a training overhead. Every manual step is time that the business is paying for that does not directly contribute to project delivery or client satisfaction. The compounding effect of those inefficiencies is what constrains growth and compresses margins.

  • Quote and project management completely separate
  • Schedule and task board disconnected from each other
  • Files stored across multiple folders with no project context
  • Variations agreed informally and only partially captured
  • Reporting assembled manually from multiple sources monthly
  • Three to five separate subscription costs running simultaneously
  • Communication scattered across email, SMS, WhatsApp and apps
  • Owners cannot see the business picture without asking multiple people

A new lead enters the CRM. The estimate is built directly inside Project Hub using the integrated Estimate Builder with itemised scope, labour, materials, markups and a professional client proposal. When the client approves, the same commercial information flows automatically into a connected project workspace. The Gantt Chart shows the planned timeline with phases and dependencies. The Job Board organises the tasks with clear owners, priorities and due dates. Documents, photos and updates attach directly to the relevant task cards. Variations are captured as entries in the commercial record with approval status and pricing. Clients receive professional SMS and email communications through the automation layer. Reports are visible in the same system without manual data assembly.

The business runs from one platform. The team does not switch between five tools with five different logins and five different learning curves. The owner can see the status of every active job without calling anyone or waiting for someone to compile a report. Admin drops because data is captured once and flows automatically to everywhere it is needed.

props.title: Built for the businesses that the big project management platforms consistently underserve
props.rightTitle: Enterprise-grade project management without the enterprise complexity, cost or implementation burden
props.rightContent:
Procore and Autodesk Construction Cloud are extraordinary platforms — for large enterprise construction companies with million-dollar IT budgets, dedicated platform administrators and full-time implementation specialists. They are sophisticated, powerful and almost completely inaccessible for the growing trade or construction business that needs genuine project management discipline without the enterprise overhead. The complexity is a feature for the businesses those platforms were designed for. For everyone else, it is a barrier that prevents adoption and creates its own cost in time and training.

Monday.com, ClickUp and Asana are capable generic work management platforms that can be configured to handle project-based workflows with enough effort and integration work. But they do not understand the trade and construction workflow natively. Quoting, estimating, materials procurement, site management and project-specific reporting require workarounds, custom configurations and third-party integrations that create their own ongoing maintenance overhead and still leave critical gaps between systems that need to share data.

Buildertrend and JobTread are purpose-built for the construction market and are genuinely useful tools — but they remain standalone project management systems that exist outside the marketing, CRM, website and communication stack that runs the commercial side of the business. You win the work somewhere else and push it into the project tool. The two worlds never properly connect.

GR8 Result Project Hub is positioned specifically for growing trade, construction and service businesses that have outgrown spreadsheets and WhatsApp management but do not need enterprise complexity. It provides genuine project management discipline connected to the sales, marketing and communication tools the business already uses, at a price point that makes sense for businesses at every stage of growth.
